verbGlossary.ADMINISTERED BY: {source} manages the contractual lifecycle of a commitment, handling amendments, compliance tracking, and closeout documentation.
verbGlossary.ALLOCATED BY: {source} distributes budget dollars across line items and cost codes according to the project estimate and scope breakdown.
verbGlossary.ANALYZED BY: {source} examines {target} or variance to identify root causes, trends, and corrective actions.
verbGlossary.APPROVED BY: {source} grants formal authorization for {target} to proceed, committing the project to the associated cost or scope.
verbGlossary.BENCHMARKED BY: {source} compares a financial metric against portfolio or industry standards to assess project performance.
verbGlossary.CERTIFIED BY: {source} attests that an invoice or pay application accurately represents completed work and is eligible for payment.
verbGlossary.CODED BY: {source} assigns cost codes and WBS codes to direct cost line items for proper cost allocation and tracking.
verbGlossary.COLLECTED BY: {source} gathers lien waivers from subcontractors and suppliers each billing cycle to protect against lien claims.
verbGlossary.DEVELOPED BY: {source} creates the original project budget from conceptual estimates, detailed takeoffs, and market pricing.
verbGlossary.DISBURSED BY: {source} executes payment transactions, routing funds from the project account to subcontractors and suppliers.
verbGlossary.DRAFTED BY: {source} prepares the initial version of a potential change order or financial document for review by others.
verbGlossary.EXECUTED BY: {source} carries out a budget modification or transfer, moving funds between line items per approved direction.
verbGlossary.FLAGGED BY: {source} identifies direct costs associated with safety incidents, rework, or non-conformance for separate tracking and analysis.
verbGlossary.FORECASTED BY: {source} projects future contingency draws or cost trends based on current burn rate and remaining scope.
verbGlossary.INCURRED BY: Field {target} authorizes or causes a direct cost to be charged against the project through field operations or resource usage.
verbGlossary.INITIATED BY: {source} creates a change event based on a field condition, design issue, or owner directive requiring cost evaluation.
verbGlossary.ISSUED BY: {source} formally releases a purchase order or commitment to a vendor, establishing a contractual obligation.
verbGlossary.MANAGED BY: {source} oversees the full lifecycle of {target} from creation through closeout, including status tracking and approvals.
verbGlossary.MONITORED BY: {source} tracks {target} or metric over time, reviewing trends and flagging deviations from plan.
verbGlossary.NEGOTIATED BY: {source} conducts commercial negotiations on pricing, scope, and terms for a commitment or change order.
verbGlossary.PREPARED BY: {source} creates a financial document (invoice, SOV, forecast) assembling data from multiple project sources.
verbGlossary.PRICED BY: {source} develops the unit costs and extended amounts for line items in a change event or commitment.
verbGlossary.PROCESSED BY: {source} routes {target} through its approval and payment workflow, ensuring completeness and compliance.
verbGlossary.PROJECTED BY: {source} creates forward-looking cash flow schedules based on billing cycles, commitment values, and payment terms.
verbGlossary.RECONCILED BY: {source} compares actual costs posted against cost codes to committed and budgeted amounts, resolving discrepancies.
verbGlossary.RECORDED BY: {source} enters a financial transaction into the project accounting system, creating the official cost record.
verbGlossary.REVIEWED BY: {source} examines {target} for accuracy, completeness, and alignment with contract terms before advancing it in workflow.
verbGlossary.SCOPED BY: {source} defines the work scope and technical requirements that a commitment will cover during preconstruction.
verbGlossary.STRUCTURED BY: {source} establishes the hierarchical code framework (cost codes, WBS) that organizes project financial data.
verbGlossary.ESTABLISHED BY: {source} develops the initial fee structure or markup percentage during preconstruction based on market conditions and project complexity.
verbGlossary.CALCULATED BY: {source} computes a financial metric or rate from underlying project data for reporting and analysis.
verbGlossary.MEASURED FOR: {source} workload or capacity metric is quantified for {target} to assess bandwidth and project allocation.
